features.summary: Deere reported strong Q3 results with 25% increase in net sales to $13 billion and 18% equipment operations margin. Production & Precision Ag segment saw 43% sales growth driven by higher production rates and price realization of 15 points. The company made significant progress reducing partially completed inventory by about one-third while achieving highest production line rates of the year. Full-year net income guidance was adjusted to $7.0-7.2 billion, reflecting higher material costs and production inefficiencies from supply disruptions.
features.management_outlook: Management expects to maintain higher production rates through Q4 and into fiscal 2023, breaking from typical seasonal patterns. Order books are full through the first half of 2023 for large ag equipment, with strong demand continuing across multiple regions including North America, Europe, and South America. The company plans to produce more large ag equipment in 2023 than 2022, supported by robust agricultural fundamentals and historically low dealer inventories. Technology adoption rates remain strong with ExactEmerge at 60% and ExactApply at 65% take rates.
features.risks: Supply chain constraints remain fragile with elevated past-due deliveries from suppliers causing production inefficiencies and higher overhead costs. The company incurred approximately $200 million in premium freight costs compared to $25 million historical average, reflecting extraordinary efforts to meet customer demand. Consumer-oriented Small Ag & Turf products are beginning to soften as they correlate more closely with general economic conditions. Geopolitical events and supply disruptions continue to impact European operations and global supply chains.
features.key_drivers: Strong agricultural fundamentals with elevated crop prices and tight global stocks-to-use ratios supporting farmer profitability and equipment replacement demand. Record low dealer inventories and above-average fleet age creating extended replacement cycle duration. Precision agriculture technology adoption accelerating globally, particularly in South America with fastest growth in engaged acres. Price realization expected to outpace material and freight costs for the full year, with list price increases in high single to low double digits for 2023 large ag products.
